---
title: "CMP specification template"
---

{{< include ../_includes/sprfmo-banner.qmd >}}

<div class="paper-title">Normative CMP Specification Template</div>

Here, **normative** means that this is the authoritative definition of the CMP:
it says what must be done. The template lists the information needed to turn a
candidate procedure into a clear and repeatable management rule. Once completed
and approved, it identifies the data, calculations, parameter values, catch
limits, exceptional-circumstances provisions, and exact calculation method to
use. Background explanations and MSE results help the Working Group choose a
CMP, but they do not change how it operates. If background text differs from
the approved specification, the approved specification takes precedence.

Use this structure for every shortlisted CMP. The approved specification tells
analysts how to calculate the advice; supporting evidence belongs in the
evidence section.

## Identity and authority

| Field | Required value |
|---|---|
| CMP identifier | Permanent name or number that is never reused |
| Version | Version number or date |
| Status | Test, shortlisted, recommended, adopted, or retired |
| Effective period | First and last management year, if applicable |
| Decision record | Body, meeting, document, date, and paragraph |
| Calculation reference | Repository, exact saved version, file, and function or control name |

## Required input data

For every input state the owner, series name, unit, spatial coverage, seasonal
coverage, annual cutoff date, revision policy, missing-value rule, and
calculation applied to it. Specify whether preliminary, revised, or final data
may be used.

## Required calculation

Document, in order:

1. data checks and exclusions;
2. calculation of the stock or index indicator;
3. indicator and reference period used by the rule;
4. HCR equation and every parameter;
5. interannual constraints;
6. rounding and numerical precision;
7. allocation among fisheries or stocks; and
8. exceptional-circumstances checks.

## Worked example

Provide a saved set of input data, intermediate values, and final advice. Add a
repeat-calculation test or digital fingerprint where useful. A reviewer must be
able to reproduce the published answer without relying on data that may later
change.

## Evidence and limitations

Link to MSE runs, operating models, performance metrics, diagnostics, and
known limitations. Evidence explains selection but does not redefine the CMP.

## Change history

For every change, state whether it only improves the wording, changes the code
without changing the answer, or changes the scientific rule. A scientific
change normally requires renewed MSE evaluation unless the approving decision
explicitly states otherwise.
